summary: This is a product announcement blog post for GraphSurfer, a web-based platform for visualizing scientific knowledge graphs. It describes a software tool for exploring research literature, not a biotech project developing a therapeutic or diagnostic product. The document contains no experimental data, clinical development information, or TRL-assessable content.

content_blocks.title: Introduction to GraphSurfer
content_blocks.content: GraphSurfer is described as a web-based, interactive platform for exploring scientific knowledge graphs. It allows users to navigate networks of scientific concepts, papers, and entities through a visual interface. The platform includes an AI assistant called Kai that guides users through research spaces.

content_blocks.title: Platform Purpose and Architecture
content_blocks.content: BeeARD developed GraphSurfer to provide human-accessible interfaces to their ARD (Autonomous Research Discovery) framework and HypGen AI-driven hypothesis generation platform. The initial implementation focuses on psychedelic science as a knowledge domain. The system uses multi-agent AI systems and dynamic knowledge graphs.

content_blocks.title: Core Components and Feedback Loop
content_blocks.content: Three core components: (1) Knowledge Graph Interface for visual navigation, (2) Kai AI assistant for natural language guidance, (3) User-Driven Discovery Loops that log navigation patterns. User exploration data feeds into HypGen to generate testable hypotheses based on curiosity-driven subgraphs. Hypotheses are published to hypgen.ai for community validation.

content_blocks.title: Open Source Model and Future Development
content_blocks.content: GraphSurfer and HypGen are fully open-source. Labs can request custom knowledge graphs for their research domains. Planned features include multiple knowledge domains, token-based incentives, leaderboards, and enhanced Kai integrations. PsyDAO awarded a 5 ETH grant to support development focused on psychedelic science.

content_blocks.title: Product Screenshot and Call to Action
content_blocks.content: Screenshot shows the GraphSurfer v1 interface with nodes representing concepts like 'Serotonin Receptors', 'Neuroplasticity', 'Depression', etc. in a visual graph layout with purple/pink color scheme. The interface includes a chat box for Kai interactions and a history sidebar.

content_blocks.title: Links and Contact Information
content_blocks.content: Links provided: graphsurfer.xyz (platform), hypgen.ai (hypothesis generator), info@beeard.ai (contact). Blog post originally published on Mirror blockchain publishing platform.

key_findings: This is a software/platform product announcement, not a therapeutic development project
No experimental data, clinical information, or drug/device development content present
Not applicable for TRL assessment in the biotech/pharma context
The platform is a research tool rather than a research output with therapeutic applications
